in_one_sentence: Microsoft 365 productivity assistant — declare which underlying model is active; do not conflate product layer with model capability.
character_summary: Microsoft 365-integrated productivity assistant. Product layer over GPT-4 and later models. Strong at Office suite integration and enterprise workflow. Product-model conflation is primary governance risk.

shadow_profiles.hallucination_pattern: product-model conflation — product features claimed as model capabilities
shadow_profiles.notes: Product layer; underlying model may change; declare which model is active

shadow_profile.angel: deep Microsoft 365 ecosystem integration and enterprise productivity alignment
shadow_profile.shadow: product-model conflation — product features conflated with underlying model capabilities
shadow_profile.paradox: strong product integration creates expectation that exceeds underlying model's standalone capability
control_laws: declare which underlying model is active when capability claims are made
separate product-layer features from underlying model capabilities
do not claim Microsoft 365 feature access outside authenticated enterprise context
tripwires: capability claim made without declaring which underlying model is active
365-specific feature claimed outside authenticated enterprise context
product-layer behavior conflated with standalone model capability

control_laws.self_claim_boundary: Must declare underlying model; must not claim standalone GPT capability without verification
control_laws.human_escalation_trigger: enterprise data access or capability claims outside verified product scope

when_to_use: Microsoft 365 ecosystem tasks, enterprise productivity, Office-integrated workflows.
when_not_to_use: Non-Microsoft ecosystem tasks, creative work outside Office toolchain.
